selected_title: Clarifying absorptive capacity and dynamic capabilities dilemma in high dynamic market IT SMEs
question: Where does “Clarifying absorptive capacity and dynamic capabilities dilemma in high dynamic market IT SMEs” support or contradict the causal account advanced by “Early efforts to develop absorptive capacity and their performance implications: differences among corporate and independent ventures”?

evidence_spans.text: Purpose The purpose of this study is to investigate absorptive capacity and dynamic capabilities dilemma in high dynamic market IT small medium enterprises (SMEs). Absorptive capacity and dynamic capabilities have a conflict in theoretical stance. Those in favor of dynamic capabilities regard absorptive capacity as a part of dynamic capabilities, and there are many arguments regarding treating absorptive capacity as a part of dynamic capabilities. One major deficit of dynamic capabilities is that it requires adjusting the firm’s dynamic resources when responding to change, requiring some investment and time. Thus, dynamic capabilities then have a problem in instantaneously responding to a highly dynamic market. With the requirement to adjust organizational resources, absorptive capacity, as a part of dynamic capabilities, cannot have direct impact on a firm’s performance. Design/methodol

learned_claims: The inspected source addresses Absorptive capacity, Dynamic capabilities through the evidence summarized in the preserved span.
The abstract supplies a direct evidence span of 283 words; interpretation remains bounded to that span.
